title: Schema editor - A JSON Schema extension for dynamic form generation
description: Meta-schema for defining editable JSON schemas with UI layout controls, custom input widgets, and conditional display logic. Extends standard JSON Schema with MediaWiki-specific widget configurations and layout options.
title: object

uiSchemaItem.description: Defines a UI schema configuration for adding dynamic properties to objects. Used with x-additional-properties-ui to allow users to add new properties at runtime.

schema.description: Reference to the JSON schema definition - must start with 'JsonSchema:MetaSchema/'

name.description: Property name identifier used when adding this item to an object

array.description: JSON Schema array type definition with extended UI options for MediaWiki form builder. Supports both simple arrays (with item schema) and tuple validation (fixed-position items).

additionalItems.description: Schema for additional items beyond those defined in prefixItems (for tuple validation)

const.description: Array must exactly equal this constant value

contains.description: Schema that at least one item in the array must satisfy

default.description: Default value for the array when not provided

items.description: Schema for array items. Use 'simple' for homogenous arrays, 'tuple' for fixed-position arrays with different types per position.

maxContains.description: Maximum number of items that can satisfy the 'contains' schema

maxItems.description: Maximum number of items allowed in the array

minContains.description: Minimum number of items that must satisfy the 'contains' schema

minItems.description: Minimum number of items required in the array

prefixItems.description: Positional validation for the beginning of the array (replaces 'items' for tuple validation in Draft 2020-12)

unevaluatedItems.description: Schema for items not evaluated by prefixItems or items

uniqueItems.description: All items in the array must be unique

arrayOptions.description: UI configuration options specific to array-type fields

x-layout.description: Layout style for array items. 'table' displays primitive items in a grid, 'tabs' shows each item in separate tabs, 'booklet' creates a step-by-step wizard, 'default' uses standard vertical list.

x-layout-config.description: Additional configuration for the selected layout (e.g., tab labels, column widths)

x-input.description: Widget type for array input. TagMultiselectWidget allows adding/removing tags, UsersMultiselectWidget for selecting wiki users, TitlesMultiselectWidget for page titles, CategoryMultiselectWidget for categories.

x-input-config.description: Configuration options passed to the selected input widget

x-control-config.description: Configuration for array control buttons (add, remove, reorder)

x-collapsible.description: Determine whether this object should be displayed as a collapsed element. Use add items "x-collapsible-config" to define options

icon.x-wikitext-description: OOUI icon name to display ([https://doc.wikimedia.org/oojs-ui/master/demos/?page=icons&theme=wikimediaui&direction=ltr&platform=desktop see here])

const.description: Boolean must exactly equal this constant value

default.description: Default boolean value when not provided

enum.description: Boolean must be one of the specified values (typically [true, false])

booleanOptions.description: UI configuration options specific to boolean-type fields

x-input.description: Widget type for boolean input. ToggleSwitchWidget shows a slider, CheckboxInputWidget shows a standard checkbox.

x-input-config.description: Configuration options passed to the selected input widget

x-control-config.description: Additional control configuration for the boolean field

commonOptions.description: Common UI options applicable to all field types

x-compact.description: Render the field in a more compact form with reduced spacing

x-css-class.description: Custom CSS class name to apply to the field container

x-dependency.description: Conditional display logic - field only shown when dependencies are met

integer.description: JSON Schema integer type definition (whole numbers only)

const.description: Integer must exactly equal this constant value

default.description: Default integer value when not provided

enum.description: Integer must be one of the specified values

exclusiveMaximum.description: Integer must be strictly less than this value

exclusiveMinimum.description: Integer must be strictly greater than this value

maximum.description: Integer must be less than or equal to this value

minimum.description: Integer must be greater than or equal to this value

multipleOf.description: Integer must be a multiple of this number

integerOptions.description: UI configuration options specific to integer-type fields

x-input.description: Widget type for integer input. NumberInputWidget provides step buttons, TextInputWidget is a plain text field.

x-input-config.description: Configuration options passed to the selected input widget

x-control-config.description: Additional control configuration for the integer field

null.description: JSON Schema null type definition - represents explicit null values

default.description: Default null value when not provided

nullOptions.description: UI configuration options for null-type fields (minimal options as null fields typically show nothing)

number.description: JSON Schema number type definition (supports integers and floating-point numbers)

const.description: Number must exactly equal this constant value

default.description: Default number value when not provided

enum.description: Number must be one of the specified values

exclusiveMaximum.description: Number must be strictly less than this value

exclusiveMinimum.description: Number must be strictly greater than this value

maximum.description: Number must be less than or equal to this value

minimum.description: Number must be greater than or equal to this value

multipleOf.description: Number must be a multiple of this value

numberOptions.description: UI configuration options specific to number-type fields

x-input.description: Widget type for number input. NumberInputWidget provides step buttons, TextInputWidget is a plain text field.

x-input-config.description: Configuration options passed to the selected input widget

x-control-config.description: Additional control configuration for the number field

object.description: JSON Schema object type definition with extended UI capabilities for dynamic property addition and nested schemas

const.description: Object must exactly equal this constant value

additionalProperties.description: Whether properties not defined in 'properties' are allowed (true) or prohibited (false)

default.description: Default object value when not provided

x-default-properties.description: List of property names that should be included by default when creating new instances

x-ui-schema.description: Alternative schema used for pattern properties when editing - provides UI for adding dynamic properties

x-ui-name.description: Name of the UI schema to display from x-additional-properties-ui list

definitions.description: Reusable schema definitions available for reference within this schema

patternProperties.description: Property validation where property names must match regex patterns

properties.description: Explicitly defined object properties with their schemas

required.description: List of property names that must be present

objectOptions.description: UI configuration options specific to object-type fields

x-layout.description: Layout style for object properties. 'tabs' organizes properties into tabbed panels, 'booklet' creates a step-by-step wizard, 'horizontal' arranges properties in a row, 'survey' displays as a questionnaire, 'default' uses standard vertical layout.

x-layout-config.description: Additional configuration for the selected layout (e.g., tab labels, column widths, step order)

x-collapsible.description: Determine whether this object should be displayed as a collapsed element. Use add items "x-collapsible-config" to define options

icon.x-wikitext-description: OOUI icon name to display ([https://doc.wikimedia.org/oojs-ui/master/demos/?page=icons&theme=wikimediaui&direction=ltr&platform=desktop see here])

x-message.description: Contextual message to display with the object field

type.description: Message severity level affecting icon and styling

icon.x-wikitext-description: OOUI icon name to display ([https://doc.wikimedia.org/oojs-ui/master/demos/?page=icons&theme=wikimediaui&direction=ltr&platform=desktop see here])

inline.description: Display message inline within the field rather than as a separate block

x-additional-properties-ui.title: UI Schema Configuration
x-additional-properties-ui.description: Defines available UI schemas for dynamically adding properties to objects. Can be a single schema definition or an array of multiple options that appear in an 'add property' dropdown.

rootOptions.description: Root-level configuration properties for the schema editor

x-name.title: name
x-name.description: Unique identifier for this schema definition. Required for storing and referencing the schema.

x-layout.description: Controls how the form fields are arranged in the UI. 'paged' splits content across multiple pages, 'tabs' organizes by tabbed panels, 'booklet' creates a step-by-step wizard, 'table' displays primitive items in grid format, 'default' uses standard vertical layout.

stringOptions.description: UI configuration options specific to string-type fields

x-input.description: Widget type for string input. Options include standard text inputs, specialized widgets for users, titles, dates, phone numbers, rich text editors, and JSON editors.

x-input-config.description: Configuration options passed to the selected input widget

x-control-config.description: Additional control configuration for the string field

search.description: API endpoint or function name for autocomplete suggestions

getResultValue.description: Function name to extract value from selected result

renderResult.description: Function name to format how results are displayed

autoSelect.description: Automatically select the first matching suggestion

schemaRoot.description: Root schema selector - determines the top-level structure. Allows choosing between Object (a set of named properties) or Array (a list of items).

schema.description: Complete JSON Schema type selector for any field. Supports all JSON Schema primitives: String, Number, Integer, Boolean, Object, Array, and Null. Each type includes appropriate validation keywords and UI configuration options.

schemaBase.description: Base JSON Schema keywords common to all schema types. Includes core validation, conditional logic, annotations, and MediaWiki-specific extensions for dynamic forms.

const.description: The value must be equal to this constant

type.description: JSON Schema primitive type (string, number, integer, boolean, object, array, null)

description.description: Human-readable description or help text for the field

$comment.description: Developer comment - ignored by validation, for documentation purposes

$id.description: Unique identifier URI for this schema

x-message.label: Attention, the type of object containing the reference must be undefined, or the type of the referenced object !!

allOf.description: Schema must be valid against all of the subschemas (logical AND)

anyOf.description: Schema must be valid against at least one of the subschemas (logical OR)

deprecated.description: Field is deprecated - show warning to users

links.description: Hyperlink relations for the schema instance

else.description: Conditional validation: must validate when 'if' does NOT validate

enum.description: The value must be one of these specified values

examples.description: Example values demonstrating valid input (not validation keywords)

if.description: Conditional validation: if this schema validates, then 'then' must validate

not.description: Schema must NOT be valid against the given schema

oneOf.description: Schema must be valid against exactly one of the subschemas (logical XOR)

readOnly.description: Field is read-only and cannot be modified

then.description: Conditional validation: must validate when 'if' validates

x-watch.description: Watched fields for reactive updates. Can be a single field alias, an array of field aliases, or key/value pairs mapping alias to field name. Changes trigger re-evaluation of dependent fields.

x-hidden.description: Hide the field from the form UI

writeOnly.description: Field can be written but not read (e.g., password fields)

schemaBaseNull.description: Base JSON Schema keywords extended for null-type schemas (subset of full schemaBase)

$id.description: Unique identifier URI for this schema

default.description: Default null value when not provided

deprecated.description: Field is deprecated - show warning to users

enum.description: The value must be one of these null values (typically [null])

writeOnly.description: Field can be written but not read

x-format.description: Special rendering format - 'info' displays as static text, 'button' renders as a button

x-button-action.description: Action name or function to execute when button is clicked (used with x-format: 'button')

string.description: JSON Schema string type definition with extended format support, enumeration sources, and template rendering

const.description: String must exactly equal this constant value

default.description: Default string value when not provided

enum.description: String must be one of the specified values (creates a dropdown or multiselect)

x-enum-titles.description: Display labels for enum values. Must be the same length as enum array. Used when enum values are codes but need human-readable labels.

x-enum-source.description: Dynamic source for enum options. Can fetch values from watched fields, static arrays, or custom API functions. Supports filtering, slicing, sorting, and custom value/title mapping.

source.description: The source of enum values. Can be: a watched field alias (string) like 'target', a static array of values (array), or the name of a registered function that returns an array of options.

anyOf.description: Alias of a watched field that will provide the array of values. Example: 'target', 'categories', 'users'
Static array of values to use as options. Example: ['Option 1', 'Option 2', 'Option 3']

title.description: Optional custom title for display. Can be a static string or a template using {{item.property}} syntax to access item properties. Example: '{{item.Vehicle type}} {{item.Make}} {{item.Model}}'

value.description: Optional custom value transformation. Can be a static string or a template using {{item.property}} syntax to access item properties. Example: '{{item._localId}}'

filter.description: Optional filter to apply to the source items. Can be: a registered function name, a string template to match against item properties, or false to disable filtering.

slice.description: Optional array of slice parameters [start, end] to limit the number of items. Example: [0, 10] takes the first 10 items.

sort.description: Optional sort order for the items. Use 'asc' for ascending or 'desc' for descending order based on the value.

x-format-type.description: Selects whether to use standard JSON Schema formats ('default') or custom MediaWiki formats ('custom')

format.description: Standard JSON Schema format validation (used when x-format-type is 'default')

x-format.description: Custom MediaWiki format type (used when x-format-type is 'custom'). Provides specialized input widgets like rating stars, color pickers, JSON editors, etc.

pattern.description: Regular expression pattern that the string must match

x-pattern-message.description: User-friendly error message to show when pattern validation fails

x-template.description: Template string for generating dynamic values (e.g., '{{field1}}-{{field2}}')
